text: After going through some heated discussions on democratic apparatus and elections at the local level in relation to the 1945 Constitution of Republic of Indonesia, several views were put forward to determine the substance of democratically elected leaders in Article 18 (4) Constitution of Republic of Indonesia. Some are of the view that voting for local representatives at the local level directly must be carried out by the people, while others hold the view that election held by DPRD also comply with the spirit of the constitution. Through constitutional analysis for the electoral arrangement of the general elections and subsequently the constitutionality of local elections (Pilkada), this study wishes to clarify that elections in the local level (Pilkada) is not the same as the democratic electoral system in the constitution as far as the DPR, DPD, President and the Vice President of Indonesia are concerned. Based on constitutional analysis of the constitution in relation to the spirit of Pancasila or the constitution of Indonesia that is based on the principles of deliberation and consensus agreed upon by founding fathers of Indonesia, it is concluded that the representative democratic system of elections in the hands of DPRD is constitutional.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
